mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned near zero lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and supports risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS remains below normal-cycle levels.
failure_condition: Reject if months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of instances or have mean returns below the unconditional mean in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% signals severe labor-market slack that depresses income growth and expected corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 8.0 have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or a higher mean return than non-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is high and the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound, policy support dominates weak labor data by compressing discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest value is 8.8 and FEDFUNDS latest value is 0.14
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition UNRATE > 8.0 and FEDFUNDS < 0.25 has a 60-day mean S&P 500 return below zero or below periods with only high unemployment and no zero-rate policy.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand annualized units indicate a weak construction and household-formation channel, reducing demand-sensitive earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 600 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below its long-run base level signals underutilized productive capacity and weak cyclical output, leading analysts to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low INDPRO observations below 95 have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their average return exceeds observations with INDPRO at or above 95.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI growth outpaces retail sales growth, nominal spending fails to translate into real demand, pressuring margins and volumes and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month percent change in CPIAUCSL exceeds 3-month percent change in RSAFS
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest value is 223.49 and RSAFS latest value is 389320.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods have a non-negative mean 20-day S&P 500 return or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and RSAFS growth has no negative out-of-sample predictive coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion during a high-unemployment regime signals early-cycle labor healing and improving household income, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite responds quickly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 8.0 and PAYEMS rises month over month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest value is 130738.0 and UNRATE latest value is 8.8
failure_condition: Reject if instances with UNRATE > 8.0 and rising PAYEMS do not outperform instances with UNRATE > 8.0 and falling PAYEMS over the next 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A CPI acceleration while the federal funds rate remains below 0.25 implies deeply negative real short rates, which supports liquidity-driven equity demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 6-month annualized CPIAUCSL growth crosses above 2.5 and FEDFUNDS < 0.25
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest value is 223.49 and FEDFUNDS latest value is 0.14
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has a 60-day mean S&P 500 return below zero or if it underperforms periods with CPIAUCSL growth below 2.5 while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25.
